items.verseEn: And has the story of Moses reached you? -

items.verse: إِنَّآ أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِۦٓ أَنْ أَنذِرْ قَوْمَكَ مِن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِيَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: Indeed, We sent Noah to his people, [saying], "Warn your people before there comes to them a painful punishment."

items.verse: وَعَلَّمَ ءَادَمَ ٱلْأَسْمَآءَ كُلَّهَا ثُمَّ عَرَضَهُمْ عَلَى ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ فَقَالَ أَنۢبِـُٔونِى بِأَسْمَآءِ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ
items.verseEn: And He taught Adam the names - all of them. Then He showed them to the angels and said, "Inform Me of the names of these, if you are truthful."

items.verse: إِذْ قَالَ يُوسُفُ لِأَبِيهِ يَـٰٓأَبَتِ إِنِّى رَأَيْتُ أَحَدَ عَشَرَ كَوْكَبًۭا وَٱلشَّمْسَ وَٱلْقَمَرَ رَأَيْتُهُمْ لِى سَـٰجِدِينَ
items.verseEn: [Of these stories mention] when Joseph said to his father, "O my father, indeed I have seen [in a dream] eleven stars and the sun and the moon; I saw them prostrating to me."

items.verse: ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَـٰهُمْ لِنَعْلَمَ أَىُّ ٱلْحِزْبَيْنِ أَحْصَىٰ لِمَا لَبِثُوٓا۟ أَمَدًۭا
items.verseEn: Then We awakened them that We might show which of the two factions was most precise in calculating what [extent] they had remained in time.

items.verse: ثُمَّ بَدَا لَهُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ مَا رَأَوُا۟ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لَيَسْجُنُنَّهُۥ حَتَّىٰ حِينٍۢ
items.verseEn: Then it appeared to them after they had seen the signs that he [i.e., al-ʿAzeez] should surely imprison him for a time.

items.verse: ثُمَّ أَنشَأْنَا مِنۢ بَعْدِهِمْ قَرْنًا ءَاخَرِينَ
items.verseEn: Then We produced after them a generation of others.

items.verse: وَإِذْ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِفَتَىٰهُ لَآ أَبْرَحُ حَتَّىٰٓ أَبْلُغَ مَجْمَعَ ٱلْبَحْرَيْنِ أَوْ أَمْضِىَ حُقُبًۭا
items.verseEn: And [mention] when Moses said to his boy [i.e., servant], "I will not cease [traveling] until I reach the junction of the two seas or continue for a long period."

items.verse: وَمَن يَكْسِبْ خَطِيٓـَٔةً أَوْ إِثْمًۭا ثُمَّ يَرْمِ بِهِۦ بَرِيٓـًۭٔا فَقَدِ ٱحْتَمَلَ بُهْتَـٰنًۭا وَإِثْمًۭا مُّبِينًۭا
items.verseEn: But whoever earns an offense or a sin and then blames it on an innocent [person] has taken upon himself a slander and manifest sin.

items.verse: فَٱعْلَمْ أَنَّهُۥ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ وَٱسْتَغْفِرْ لِذَنۢبِكَ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ مُتَقَلَّبَكُمْ وَمَثْوَىٰكُمْ
items.verseEn: So know, [O Muḥammad], that there is no deity except Allāh and ask forgiveness for your sin and for the believing men and believing women. And Allāh knows of your movement and your resting place.

items.verse: مِّمَّا خَطِيٓـَٔـٰتِهِمْ أُغْرِقُوا۟ فَأُدْخِلُوا۟ نَارًۭا فَلَمْ يَجِدُوا۟ لَهُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ أَنصَارًۭا
items.verseEn: Because of their sins they were drowned and put into the Fire, and they found not for themselves besides Allāh [any] helpers.

items.verse: لَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُنَاحٌ أَن تَبْتَغُوا۟ فَضْلًۭا مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۚ فَإِذَآ أَفَضْتُم مِّنْ عَرَفَـٰتٍۢ فَٱذْكُرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ عِندَ ٱلْمَشْعَرِ ٱلْحَرَامِ ۖ وَٱذْكُرُوهُ كَمَا هَدَىٰكُمْ وَإِن كُنتُم مِّن قَبْلِهِۦ لَمِنَ ٱلضَّآلِّينَ
items.verseEn: There is no blame upon you for seeking bounty from your Lord [during ḥajj]. But when you depart from ʿArafāt, remember Allāh at al-Mashʿar al-Ḥarām. And remember Him, as He has guided you, for indeed, you were before that among those astray.

categories.name: مَفْعُولَاتُ فِعْلِ الْإِلْقَاءِ
categories.nameEn: Recipients of the Act of Casting

items.verse: فَأُلْقِىَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ سُجَّدًۭا قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا بِرَبِّ هَـٰرُونَ وَمُوسَىٰ
items.verseEn: So the magicians fell down in prostration. They said, "We have believed in the Lord of Aaron and Moses."

items.verse: سَنُلْقِى فِى قُلُوبِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ٱلرُّعْبَ بِمَآ أَشْرَكُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ مَا لَمْ يُنَزِّلْ بِهِۦ سُلْطَـٰنًۭا ۖ وَمَأْوَىٰهُمُ ٱلنَّارُ ۚ وَبِئْسَ مَثْوَى ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ
items.verseEn: We will cast terror into the hearts of those who disbelieve for what they have associated with Allāh of which He had not sent down [any] authority. And their refuge will be the Fire, and wretched is the residence of the wrongdoers.

items.verse: وَقَالَتِ ٱلْيَهُودُ يَدُ ٱللَّهِ مَغْلُولَةٌ ۚ غُلَّتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَلُعِنُوا۟ بِمَا قَالُوا۟ ۘ بَلْ يَدَاهُ مَبْسُوطَتَانِ يُنفِقُ كَيْفَ يَشَآءُ ۚ وَلَيَزِيدَنَّ كَثِيرًۭا مِّنْهُم مَّآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِن رَّبِّكَ طُغْيَـٰنًۭا وَكُفْرًۭا ۚ وَأَلْقَيْنَا بَيْنَهُمُ ٱلْعَدَٰوَةَ وَٱلْبَغْضَآءَ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۚ كُلَّمَآ أَوْقَدُوا۟ نَارًۭا لِّلْحَرْبِ أَطْفَأَهَا ٱللَّهُ ۚ وَيَسْعَوْنَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَسَادًۭا ۚ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ
items.verseEn: And the Jews say, "The hand of Allāh is chained." Chained are their hands, and cursed are they for what they say. Rather, both His hands are extended; He spends however He wills. And that which has been revealed to you from your Lord will surely increase many of them in transgression and disbelief. And We have cast among them animosity and hatred until the Day of Resurrection. Every time they kindled the fire of war [against you], Allāh extinguished it. And they strive throughout the land [causing] corruption, and Allāh does not like corrupters.

items.verse: أَلْقِيَا فِى جَهَنَّمَ كُلَّ كَفَّارٍ عَنِيدٍۢ
items.verseEn: [Allāh will say], "Throw into Hell every obstinate disbeliever,

harf.hint: A divine attribute that encompasses all of creation.
harf.verse: 19:2 — [This is] a mention of the mercy of your Lord to His servant Zechariah

deduction.title: The Call for a Heavenly Feast
deduction.intro: A group of devoted followers stood by their prophet, their faith tested by a profound request. They sought a sign not from doubt, but to strengthen their hearts, asking for a miracle that would descend from the heavens and serve as a testament for all generations. Who were these companions?
clues: We were the close companions and helpers of a prophet sent to the Children of Israel.
We famously pledged our allegiance, declaring, 'We are the supporters of Allah'.
Our prophet was given the ability to perform great miracles, like healing the blind and lepers, and giving life to the dead by Allah's permission.
Despite our strong belief, we made a bold request for a tangible sign to further reassure our hearts.
We specifically asked our prophet to pray for a table laden with food to be sent down to us from the heavens.
Our request was met with a divine promise, but also a stern warning: if any of us disbelieved after this sign, a punishment would come like no other.

identity.label: Who Are We?
options: The Companions of the Cave (Ashab al-Kahf)
The People of the Ditch (Ashab al-Ukhdud)
The Disciples of 'Isa (Al-Hawariyyun)
The Ansar of Madinah
The Companions of Musa

trial.label: What Was Our Request?
options: To see Allah manifestly
A table spread with food from heaven
For a she-camel to emerge from a mountain
To be granted eternal life in this world
For a river to gush forth from a rock
trial.answer: A table spread with food from heaven

location.label: What Was the Stated Purpose?
options: To hoard provisions for themselves
To challenge the prophet's authority
To prove their superiority over other nations
To reassure their hearts and be witnesses
To gain worldly power and kingship
location.answer: To reassure their hearts and be witnesses

outcome.label: What Was the Divine Response?
options: The request was outright denied
An immediate punishment of a storm
A promise to send it with a stern warning
They were turned into apes and swine
A spring of water was provided instead
outcome.answer: A promise to send it with a stern warning

deduction.arabic: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ كُونُوٓا۟ أَنصَارَ ٱللَّهِ كَمَا قَالَ عِيسَى ٱبْنُ مَرْيَمَ لِلْحَوَارِيِّـۧنَ مَنْ أَنصَارِىٓ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ قَالَ ٱلْحَوَارِيُّونَ نَحْنُ أَنصَارُ ٱللَّهِ ۖ فَـَٔامَنَت طَّآئِفَةٌۭ مِّنۢ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ وَكَفَرَت طَّآئِفَةٌۭ ۖ فَأَيَّدْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ عَلَىٰ عَدُوِّهِمْ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ ظَـٰهِرِينَ
deduction.verse: O you who have believed, be supporters of Allāh, as when Jesus, the son of Mary, said to the disciples, "Who are my supporters for Allāh?" The disciples said, "We are supporters of Allāh." And a faction of the Children of Israel believed and a faction disbelieved. So We supported those who believed against their enemy, and they became dominant. (61:14)

translations: and the Last and the Apparent
scramble.hint: This verse describes four of Allah's magnificent attributes, encompassing all of time and existence.
scramble.arabic: هُوَ ٱلْأَوَّلُ وَٱلْـَٔاخِرُ وَٱلظَّـٰهِرُ وَٱلْبَاطِنُ ۖ وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌ
scramble.verseEn: He is the First and the Last, the Ascendant and the Intimate, and He is, of all things, Knowing.

verse.arabic_text: وَمَا مِن دَآبَّةٍ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ إِلَّا عَلَى ٱللَّهِ رِزْقُهَا وَيَعْلَمُ مُسْتَقَرَّهَا وَمُسْتَوْدَعَهَا ۚ كُلٌّ فِى كِتَٰبٍ مُّبِينٍ
verse.translation: There is no moving creature on earth whose provision is not guaranteed by Allah. And He knows where it lives and where it is laid to rest. All is ˹written˺ in a clear Record.

theme_question.correct: Allah's absolute sovereignty and His guarantee of sustenance for every living being.
options: The specific laws of inheritance and family obligations.
Allah's absolute sovereignty and His guarantee of sustenance for every living being.
The importance of fasting and pilgrimage for spiritual purification.
The historical account of the prophets' battles against disbelievers.

educational_notes.verse_context: This verse is the opening statement of Juz 12, setting a powerful tone. It comes within Surah Hud, which addresses the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) and the believers, offering solace and strength by recounting the stories of previous prophets who faced rejection. This verse serves as a foundational reminder that the Creator who provides for every creature will surely support His messengers and believers.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The central theme is Tawakkul (trust in Allah) rooted in the understanding of His attribute as Ar-Razzaq (The Provider). The verse negates any doubt about sustenance, affirming that Allah has not only decreed provision for every single creature but also possesses perfect knowledge of its entire life cycle—its dwelling place on earth ('mustaqarraha') and its final resting place after death ('mustawda'aha'). This comprehensive knowledge, recorded in a "Clear Record" (Kitabin Mubeen), underscores His absolute control and meticulous planning, encouraging believers to trust His plan without anxiety.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah Hud, named after the Prophet Hud, is a Meccan surah revealed during a period of intense persecution of the early Muslims, known as the "Year of Sorrow." The surah's primary focus is to console the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) and fortify the believers' faith by narrating the struggles of prophets like Nuh (Noah), Hud, Salih, Ibrahim (Abraham), Lut (Lot), Shu'ayb, and Musa (Moses). A recurring theme is the stubborn rejection of divine guidance by their people and the subsequent divine justice. The surah emphasizes the importance of steadfastness (istiqamah) and warns against associating partners with Allah.
